comment_text:
I have a theory, Young-hee is the red light green light doll and in korean elementary school books (back in the 1930s) Young-Hee and her brother Chul-su were fictional characters who would teach them about morals and etc. 
Most of the games are based off korean childhood games, so this makes sense.
But now, we have a second doll. Chul-Su, Young-hee’s brother. My theory is that Chul-Su will stand behind the starting line so the players cant hide and Young-hee will stay behind the finish line, where she always was.
So i think that in s3 it will be the same games as season one or a mix of s1 and s2 but WAY more brutal and challenging.

Thanks for reading this meaningless message :3

comment_text:
I have a theory about the new game. Do you remember when the shaman threw a curse on all the main characters ? She said they will see all other people suffer before their die. I think all players we love (like the grandma or the 120) will die in this game. Also, we can see there is an opposition between the two dolls and the sky. One side, it's the night and in the other it's the day... maybe the X and O players will compete in this game. Unfortunately I think the X players will lose. Maybe some players will survive like the 222, 333 or the 007 because they could vote to stay for some reason.
In this case 456 will surely vote to stay because he no longer believes in him, he no longer thinks he can save everyone after the death of his friend, so he will survive to the next game.
About the game, it's surely a revised version of red light green light. Each team has its own doll and must cross to the other side, the twist is that the opposing players will want to push each other since they will cross each other. We can also see that the land is smaller, but I have no explanation.
On my opinion, this game will be very sad like the marble game of the past season. After, the only players remaining will vote to stay, it will be a major turning point in the story. 
Especially since the VIPs arrive after this game, so the organisers must be sure that it will continue.
What do you think?
